algorithms.title: Prim's Algorithm (MST)
algorithms.description: Prim's algorithm finds the Minimum Spanning Tree of a weighted undirected graph. It starts from any vertex and greedily adds the cheapest edge that connects a visited vertex to an unvisited one. Uses a min-heap (priority queue) for efficiency. Time: O(E log V).
algorithms.intuition: Build the MST one vertex at a time. Start with any vertex. At each step, pick the minimum-weight edge that crosses the frontier (visited → unvisited). The greedy choice works because of the cut property: the minimum-weight edge crossing any cut belongs to some MST.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Initialize: add start vertex to visited set, add all its edges to min-heap."},{"step":2,"description":"While heap not empty: poll minimum edge (weight, to)."},{"step":3,"description":"If 'to' already visited, skip (would create cycle)."},{"step":4,"description":"Mark 'to' as visited, add edge weight to MST total."},{"step":5,"description":"Add all edges from 'to' to unvisited neighbors into the heap."}]
algorithms.timeComplexity: O(E log V) with priority queue

variants.desc: Sort all edges by weight, add if it doesn't form a cycle (Union-Find)

algorithms.title: Kruskal's Algorithm (MST)
algorithms.description: Kruskal's algorithm finds the MST by sorting all edges by weight and adding them greedily using Union-Find to detect cycles. Different from Prim's: Kruskal processes edges globally (sorted), Prim grows from a starting vertex. Kruskal is often preferred for sparse graphs.
algorithms.intuition: Sort all edges by weight. Process cheapest first. Add an edge if it connects two different components (no cycle). Union-Find tells us in near O(1) whether two vertices are already connected. The greedy choice: always picking the cheapest safe edge builds the MST.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Sort all edges by weight ascending."},{"step":2,"description":"Initialize Union-Find with V singleton components."},{"step":3,"description":"For each edge (u, v, w) in sorted order:"},{"step":4,"description":"If find(u) != find(v): add edge to MST, union(u, v), add weight."},{"step":5,"description":"Stop when MST has V-1 edges."}]
algorithms.timeComplexity: O(E log E) — dominated by sorting

useCases.title: Sparse graph MST
useCases.desc: More efficient than Prim's when E << V²

useCases.title: Road network planning
useCases.desc: Connect N cities with minimum total road length

variants.title: Reverse Kruskal (Maximum Spanning Tree)
variants.desc: Sort edges descending to find maximum spanning tree

variants.title: Kruskal with path compression
variants.desc: Union by rank + path compression for near O(1) union-find
